reviews.review_title: It's a good game loved what I could
reviews.review_description: The game keeps crashing can't make it past the firing range and I really want to play I'm on the quest 3

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear. What headset are you playing on?

reviews.review_description:
First off keep in mind this game is early access so there are a few issues.  Once these are ironed out I believe this may be one of my favorite standalone horror games.  
  You quickly know your role and I love the use of the vhs to deliever the story.  The audio keeps a suspenseful atmosphere while the jump scares keep you on your toes.  
  The gameplay was smooth and it was fun to go through the puzzles especially looking for the fuses.  Great work to the devs and looking forward to see things get fine tuned.

developer_response.body: Thank you for this. We want to take this core idea and then expand it for a much longer and more fleshed out (pun intended) game. We want to create a truly engaging VR horror game that will keep people guessing.

reviews.review_description:
This game made me jump more than anything else has in vr and I have played most of the horror games 

Really hope the team can finish this game it's going to be fantastic

developer_response.body: Thanks for this. We do intend to do more on this game in the near future. Thanks for the support!

reviews.review_title: Get ready to be hooked in minutes...
reviews.review_description:
What an intriguing and captivating game! Right from the start, you clearly understand your character, the situation, your motivation, and the unfolding plot. The game mechanics are seamlessly executed, and the pacing is spot on. The Dev Studio masterfully immerses you in their world, hooking you within minutes and leaving you eager to uncover more and make it out... alive.
They also have released "The Atlas Mystery", check it out too!!!

developer_response.body: Thank you for this support and kind words. We want to make this the best immersive horror game on the market.

reviews.review_title: There's one part that keeps killing me
reviews.review_description:
So I just watched the original Mulam movie, and although I wasn't a fan of it personally, I took a look at it and finished it anyway since I know this game is based off of that. Anyway, I do think this game is SEMI-Scary, I do think that the gun play could be a bit better. I don't like the fact that I could get a headshot and they don't collapse immediately. I do think that the whole cult plot disturbing, I do appreciate that the game isn't hyper emotional and dramatic like the movie, because Jesus Christ there are some parts of the movie that I am so glad aren't in this game.

OK NOW TO TALK ABOUT THE PART THAT I HAVE BECOME OBSESSED WITH...

There's a part where after you check all of the exits, and shoot one of the cultists and play the tape she dropped, you have to go downstairs into the basement and have your gun loaded, and then... we get to the part where I keep dying. It's not the game's fault, it's not a really difficult part and I just suck at the game, no... It's the fact that there's an NPC that's dressed in a super attractive outfit, that being a crop top and skinny jean shorts. Yeah I keep dying here on purpose because of how attractive she looks, and it's not just that but it's also the fact that she's basically a really attractive female slasher just like her two friends she's chatting with. And the fact that this is in VR really doesn't help either.

Without getting too inappropriate, all I will say is this... When I am playing a game or watching a movie that's supposed to be scary, having a super attractive killer that's dressed like she's out to get men at the bar to stare at her, to the point where she looks like she would be PERFECT for Nightclub Simulator VR, it kind of defeats the purpose of this being a horror game. DEFINITELY LEAVE HER IN THE GAME THOUGH!

I guess it does make it more psychologically scarier because if she was not dressed up like that and perhaps dressed up in a really scary outfit, it would make me wanna shoot her, but it looks like she's dressed up to make me wanna just let her do whatever she wants to me.

AWESOME EXPERIENCE!

developer_response.body: Hey! We're glad you enjoyed the experiences on multiple levels :-)

reviews.review_title: This game's going to be good..
reviews.review_description: I bought this a few back played around with it but was waiting for it to be fine-tuned before I finished playing it and gave it a review it's starting to run nice and look really nice looking good so far runs a lot better than it did at the beginning wish there was some control options though..

developer_response.body: Thank you so much. We are working on some improvements and appreciate the support. I'm glad you noticed some of the changes we've made of late and stay tuned for more!

reviews.review_description: Nice game but why is it so short, I want more, when it's finished?

developer_response.body: Thank you! Games are hard and expensive. We're working on ideas and will have more soon :-)

reviews.review_description:
Very Good Game with Fantastic Graphics, Ambient Sound, Music and an ABSOLUTELY FRIGHTENING sense of place.
It's scary kids ... Very scary !!!
Go slow, save your ammo and tread carefully....  
As it is it's a bit short so adding  another floor or two in the abandoned Police Station would be nice/scary to explore and maybe a few new puzzles to solve too !!!   (Even hiding the fuses in different random places each time you play would add to the replay.)  In anycase, as it is now it's FANTASTIC...  (But only if you like that pit like feeling in your gut.)

developer_response.body: Thanks Jim! We're going to add some new rooms to the game quite soon

reviews.review_title: Potential to be fav VR horror title
reviews.review_description:
Jessica Lauren's first day working at Landford Police Department cannot get any worse. 
Your father was an Ex cop at the same station who ended up going insane after a case.
Something strange is happening and it's all tied to the Cult that the game is named after.

As you explore the department and just about settle into your first nightshift things take a turn for the worse; when strange happenings start and you find cult members have locked you in the station.

You explore the station in and solve relatively grounded tasks (eg change some fuses) unlike the crazy puzzles in say the Resident Evil series.
All you need is a keen eye to find things and a steadier aim to take shots at flock members intending to kill you.

The game expertly weaves footage from the film Malum in the form of VHS tapes to help fill you in on the story.

The game's introduction is masterfully played out with the excellent sound direction giving a sense of unease and dread.
There are just enough jumpscares to prevent you from ever getting too comfortable but the game excels at building an uncomfortable eerie atmosphere.

However, you have to remember that the game is still currently in Early Access (and ends in a cliffhanger)
But if the developers can match expand what is found in the game already and finish off the story.....I say it has the potential to knock "Propagation Paradise Hotel" as my fav VR horror game (on a standalone VR device)

Haven't finished it yet because I'm afraid to keep playing. I will though because the story is really good and I definitely have a good amount to play. Scariest game I've ever played in my life. I had one issue where the game was crashing for me, but restarting my headset fixed the issue. I think my headset was getting scared too.

developer_response.body:
ha, this is great! Thanks! 

We run the memory really close to the edge on this one and so if you've been using the headset for a while and running other apps, it may crash. Best to reset. 

We are working on this as we optimize the game.

reviews.review_title: Promising, but beware the adrupt ending.
reviews.review_description:
Just a warning for potential buyers, just as the price tag woukd suggeat, this is literally nothing but basically tge VERY beginning of a game. It's basically equel to playing through a tutorial of another survival horror title at the moment, so the story goes NOWHERE but droping some crumbs, only to end with a "To be Continued" when the story didn't even really begin and you have done very VERY little gameplay-wise (though  its still early access + $10).

That said, what is there is fun and well realized, though controls could use some tweaking (though not much honestly). 
I cant wait to see what more yhey do, I just hope they add on to THIS game rather than another 20 minute sequel later.
I completely recommend his to horror fans though, just keep n mind what you're getting.
It seems like it can be something great if it continues being worked on and massively added on to with the same amount of care that seems to have gone into developing it to where it is currently. As a life-long Survival-Horror game enthusiast, Flock has an excellent look and overall feel to it, though the gameplay along with other small things does need work.

-Grabbing some items in low areas is impossible unless you are using the in-gsme crouch, as they become uninteractable if you are only physically crouching, but luckily the crouch button is faster and easier anyway. Just something to note.

- your character slows back down and gets out of breath acting as if she'll pass out just from you slowly running for MAYBE 14 feet..

developer_response.body:
UPDATED:

Hi James - we've made a number of changes in v1.4 and now 1.5.1 that address some of your notes. I'd love for you to play it again and give us some feedback!

reviews.review_description:
But when it gets to the title screen of the game, it crashes  and constantly takes me back to the oculus menu. I'm using oculus quest 2
UPDATED: Is there a specific way to kill Betty or keep avoiding her till I find the fuse? I mean I looked everywhere in the morgue, but still can't find it! maybe you should give me the answer to where it's located, thanks dev

developer_response.body:
Keep searching the morgue! If you can get past Betty :-)

Update - check lower down for the other fuse!

reviews.review_description:
Video gameplay and impressions here (remove the space before the 'be':

https://youtu. be/qkyopmbku74?si=P9mUBq8GyrPUbR_X

Flock of the Low God has a very effective atmosphere and scares, it's a truly well-made horror game that clearly had a lot of hard work put into it! Highly recommended!

developer_response.body: Wow! Thank you so much. That means a lot as we did put a lot of work into generating a tense atmosphere for the player. Glad you enjoyed. :-)

reviews.review_title: A delight for any horror fan
reviews.review_description: The developers here did a great job telling you a story while scaring the hell outta you. Feel like the character in a movie turning around when they shouldn’t, opening doors they shouldn’t, and going down halls they shouldn’t. Great stuff!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much. It is our intent to SCARE and SCARE we have... :-)

reviews.review_description:
If you love some good horror, then this game is for you.  It seemed to encompass everything in a horror game you would want to see.  
A well written story, a fantastic sound score and smooth gameplay.  
Let’s not forget well placed jump scares that even though you know they are coming you still jump.   
This team really understands and appreciates the value of good psychological horror. I cannot wait for more to come and to be scared to death.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! We appreciate your kind thoughts and recognition of all of our effort to create this game in the best possible way.

reviews.review_description: The game is really scary and fun.

developer_response.body: Amazing. Thanks so much for your support.

reviews.review_description: I absolutely love this game. I’m stuck at one part but it makes me try that much harder to get past it. The music is super creepy and the tension mounts at every corner. The people from “the cult” are super scary and creepy as well. The controls work great and I have a blast playing it. Another definite hidden gem in Applab!!
Feels like being immersed in a horror movie. The environments are creepy and I enjoyed piecing together the story. The scares were very effective and I enjoyed the mix of action and horror.

developer_response.body: Thank you! That was our goal!

reviews.review_description: The game had some scares kept me jumping and looking over my shoulder.  The story and voice acting is pretty good and explained why you were there and kept you engaged.  Warning the scares are real and the games is intense at times

developer_response.body: Thank you! That was our intention and we really appreciate your support!

reviews.review_description:
This was such a fun game! I had zero expectations and was taken for a thrilling ride. The liminal space of the decommissioned police station was captured perfectly, with nuggets of detail that weave the scenery into your reality and pique your curiosity. You find yourself totally fine with being murdered because you really want to find out what happened. Super cool storyline!

The music was perfection, so creepy! Great use of texture to set the scene. Loved the homage with Case No. 504, nice touch. I especially loved the VHS chapter setup. Whoever came up with that is amazing.

Gameplay:
I rather enjoyed the realistic nature of the weapon functionality. I had to let go of being spoiled with being told how much ammo I had with expectations of an unlimited ammo cache and actually pay attention to it.

I think we all know the favorite part. For me, what was thrilling about Betty is that I knew I needed to keep looking but I was trying to overcome my fear of her to be able to focus on what I was doing. When you watch scary movies, you always wonder how you’ll perform in the situations. At least now I know I’d fail horribly. 

Feedback:
An option to skip parts of the story that you’ve already seen would be helpful. As this was our first horror game in VR, it took some…getting used to. Basically, we are a bunch of ninnies and kept taking the headset off at first. Often, unless we went right back in, this resulted in having to repeat parts several times. We did, however, appreciate the automatic save points within the chapters.

There were some initial challenges with interacting with needed objects, particularly ones on the floor, whether placed there or had gotten knocked off a higher surface. I found reaching for things and willing them into my hand like I was a Jedi worked well, but other players had more realistic expectations. I also found that enlarging the boundary helped, so perhaps there’s a tolerance adjustment that can be done?


Overall, well done. I bought Meta Quest 3 as a shared bday present for my son and me and this was one of the first games we played. It has me very, very excited about our new VR journey. I genuinely cannot wait for Chapter 5!!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for this extremely detailed and kind review! We're working on the next chapter and improving the game and we'll definitely look at your feedback as we craft the next freaky installment :--)
